ground_truth:
App: **Jan** (v0.8.2). **Three columns**: left nav sidebar + middle settings-category column (e.g. Appearance/Assistants-style entries) + right settings content. Top-right corner has the macOS traffic-light dots **plus extra icons** (download/grid/split-view). Provider-list entries carry **colored per-provider icons**.
- **Left nav**: window-control dots; items `New Chat` `⌘N`, `New Projects` `⌘P`, `Search` `⌘K`, `Hub`, `Settings` (active) — **keyboard shortcuts ARE shown** next to these items. Sections `Chats` (recent: "Build a PacMan Clone…", "Translate the following…", "interpreter"), `INTEGRATIONS` (`Experimental` tag, `MCP Servers`, `Claude Code`), `MODEL PROVIDERS` (`+` add; `LOCAL`: `Llama.cpp`, `MLX`; `REMOTE`: `OpenAI`, `Azure`, `Anthropic`, `OpenRouter`, `Mistral`, `Groq` (last fully-visible entry), `x.AI` (last entry, **truncated** — only the top ~40% visible; models often render it "xAI")).
- **Right (Settings)**: sections `General` (`App Version` `v0.8.2`; `Automatic Update Check` toggle **on** + button `Check for Updates` **enabled**; `Language` dropdown `English`), `Data Folder` (`App Data` path `/Users/frederic/Library/Application Support/Jan/data` + `Change Location`; `App Logs` + `Show in Finder` / `Open Logs`), `Advanced` (`Jan CLI` at `/Users/frederic/.local/bin/jan` + `Uninstall`; `Reset To Factory Settings` + red `Reset` button).

responses.content:
### (1) Overall Layout

- Three-column layout: left sidebar (global navigation), middle sidebar (settings categories), right panel (settings content).
- Top bar contains window control buttons (red, yellow, green) and two icons (download, grid).
- Scrollable areas in middle and right panels; left sidebar is fixed.

### (2) Visible Components with Verbatim Text Labels

- Window controls: red circle, yellow circle, green circle
- Icons: download icon, grid icon
- Left sidebar items:
  - New Chat
  - New Projects
  - Search
  - Hub
  - Settings
  - Chats
  - Build a PacMan Clone ...
  - Translate the following...
  - interpreter
- Middle sidebar items:
  - General
  - Appearance
  - Assistants
  - Attachments
  - Local API Server
  - HTTPS Proxy
  - Shortcuts
  - Hardware
  - Privacy
  - INTEGRATIONS (with Experimental badge)
  - MCP Servers
  - Claude Code
  - MODEL PROVIDERS
  - LOCAL
  - Llama.cpp
  - MLX
  - REMOTE
  - OpenAI
  - Azure
  - Anthropi
  - OpenRouter
  - Mistral
  - Groq
  - xAI (partially visible)
- Right panel sections:
  - General
  - App Version → v0.8.2
  - Automatic Update Check → Automatically check for updates on startup and periodically.
  - Check for Updates → Check if a newer version of Jan is available.
  - Language → English
  - Data Folder
  - App Data → Location for messages, downloaded models and other data.
  - /Users/frederic/Library/Application Support/Jan/data
  - App Logs → View detailed logs of the App.
  - Advanced
  - Jan CLI → Installed at /Users/frederic/.local/bin/jan — use jan from your terminal to serve models.
  - Reset To Factory Settings → Restore application to its initial state, erasing all models and chat history. This action is irreversible and recommended only if the application is corrupted.

### (3) Feedback, Status, or Error Messages

- No active feedback, status, or error messages visible.
- “Experimental” badge next to “INTEGRATIONS” indicates experimental feature status.
- Warning text under “Reset To Factory Settings”: “This action is irreversible and recommended only if the application is corrupted.”

### (4) Interactive Elements and Their State

- Window controls: red (close), yellow (minimize), green (maximize) — standard macOS behavior.
- Download and grid icons — likely functional but no state shown.
- Toggle switch for “Automatic Update Check” — ON (orange).
- Button: “Check for Updates” — clickable, default state.
- Dropdown: “Language” — set to “English”, arrow indicates expandable.
- Button: “Change Location” — clickable, default state.
- Path field: `/Users/frederic/Library/Application Support/Jan/data` — editable? No edit indicator; likely read-only with copy icon.
- Copy icon next to path — clickable.
- Buttons: “Show in Finder”, “Open Logs” — clickable, default state.
- Button: “Uninstall” — clickable, default state.
- Button: “Reset” — red, prominent, likely destructive action.
- Plus icon next to “MODEL PROVIDERS” — likely adds new provider.
- Three-dot menu next to “Chats” — likely expands options.
- All buttons appear in default (non-pressed) state except toggle and reset button which are active/destined.
